                           Introduction of Seasonally Adjusted Job Vacancy
                                        Data for Manufacturing

                                                                                                                      *   by Robert P. Pepson




                   Beginning with this issue, the seasonally adjusted          Seasonally adjusted job vacancy rates are computed by
                number and rate of job vacancies for manufacturing             dividing the seasonally adjusted number of job vacancies
                will be published regularly. The series published include      by the sum of seasonally adjusted employment and job
                both total and long-term job vacancies.* (See table E-2        vacancies and multiplying the quotient by 100. Season-
                on page 112). The more than three years of experience          ally adjusted long-term job vacancy rates are computed
                in the collection of job vacancy data indicate that both       by dividing the seasonally adjusted long-term job vacan-
                the total and long-term vacancy series exhibit a sub-          cies by the sum of seasonally adjusted employment and
                stantial amount of seasonality.                                total job vacancies and multiplying the quotient by 100.
                   The Bureau of Labor Statistics publishes seasonally         All seasonal computations are based on unrounded data.
                adjusted as well as unadjusted figures, because data are           The accompanying table presents the seasonal factors
                used for different purposes by different groups. For           for the current adjustment of the job vacancy series.
                analyzing general labor trends in the economy, seasonally      These factors will be revised at the time the industry
                adjusted data are generally preferred since they eliminate     employment statistics are adjusted to March 1971
                the effect of changes that normally occur at the same          benchmarks.
                time and in about the same magnitude every year.
                   The seasonally adjusted series on the number of total           * Of the division of Industry Employment Statistics, Office
                                                                               of Current Employment Analysis.
                and long-term job vacancies are computed by applying               1
                                                                                      For definitions and a detailed discussion of job vacancies
                factors directly to the corresponding unadjusted series.       see Technical Note, page 129.




                 Seasonal adjustment factors for the number of job vacancies in manufacturing

                COLLECTION                                                                    All national, State, and area employment, hours, earnings,

                    Payroll reports provide current information on wage and               ance with the Standard Industrial Classification Manual, Bureau

                    Under cooperative arrangements with State agencies, the               any part of the pay period which includes the 12th of the

                turnover reporting form, which is then used for national, State,          figures represent the number of persons who occupied positions

                    Two types of data collection schedules are used: Form BLS             employed are persons who are laid off, on leave without
                790-Monthly Report on Employment, Payroll, and Hours;                     pay, or on strike for the entire period or who are hired but

                Labor 'Turnover. These schedules are of the "shuttle" type,

                that the next month's data can be entered. This procedure                 Industry hours and earnings
